source:
## 11 · Training Data Augmentation: v8 and v8b

With 6,200 clean OpenCRE pairs available, the question becomes how to fold external signal into training without degrading the classifier. Two approaches were tried. **v8** used disagreement mining: run the v7c classifier on all 6,200 pairs, keep the 2,000 where the model was most uncertain, and add the 673 it classified incorrectly. **v8b** took a more direct route: add 2,046 OpenCRE pairs across three tiers, capped per class, and sweep three transformer backbones. Both experiments exposed fatal issues—DeBERTa-large collapsed under noisy labels, and the LightGBM stacker memorized the small feature matrix—leading to v_final, which abandons OpenCRE training data entirely.

# 11.1 — Load training splits and diagnosis files
TIER_NAMES = ["UNRELATED", "PARTIAL", "RELATED", "EQUIVALENT"]

def load_jsonl(path):
    with open(path) as f:
        return [json.loads(line) for line in f]

expert_train = load_jsonl(REPO / "data" / "splits" / "expert_train.jsonl")
v8_train     = load_jsonl(REPO / "data" / "splits" / "v8_train.jsonl")
v8b_train    = load_jsonl(REPO / "data" / "splits" / "v8b_train.jsonl")

print(f"expert_train: {len(expert_train):,} records")
print(f"v8_train:     {len(v8_train):,} records")
print(f"v8b_train:    {len(v8b_train):,} records")

# Tier distributions
for name, data in [("expert_train", expert_train), ("v8_train", v8_train), ("v8b_train", v8b_train)]:
    dist = Counter(r["tier_label"] for r in data)
    parts = [f"{TIER_NAMES[k]}={dist[k]:,}" for k in sorted(dist)]
    print(f"  {name:14s} tiers: {', '.join(parts)}")

# Load diagnosis files
v8_diag = json.loads((REPO / "runs" / "v8_diagnosis" / "v8_data_assembly.json").read_text())
v8b_diag = json.loads((REPO / "runs" / "v8b_diagnosis" / "v8b_data_assembly.json").read_text())

print(f"\nv8 disagreement mining:")
print(f"  Clean pairs: {v8_diag['clean']:,}  |  Disagreements: {v8_diag['disagreements']:,}  |  Selected: {v8_diag['selected']:,}  |  Added: {v8_diag['v8_rows_added']}")
print(f"\nv8b direct augmentation:")
print(f"  Clean pairs: {v8b_diag['clean']:,}  |  Added: {v8b_diag['v8_rows_added']:,}  |  Skipped by cap: {v8b_diag['skipped_by_cap']:,}")

# Figure 11.1 — Training set composition: tier distribution across splits
TIER_PALETTE_LIST = ["#6096ba", "#8ac926", "#f4a261", "#e76f51"]

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 4.5), sharey=True)

for ax, (name, data, title) in zip(axes, [
    ("expert_train", expert_train, "expert_train (5,920)"),
    ("v8_train", v8_train, "v8_train (12,849)"),
    ("v8b_train", v8b_train, "v8b_train (14,222)"),
]):
    dist = Counter(r["tier_label"] for r in data)
    counts = [dist.get(i, 0) for i in range(4)]
    bars = ax.bar(TIER_NAMES, counts, color=TIER_PALETTE_LIST, edgecolor="white", width=0.6)
    for bar, val in zip(bars, counts):
        ax.text(
            bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
            bar.get_height() + 40,
            f"{val:,}",
            ha="center", va="bottom", fontweight="bold", fontsize=9,
        )
    ax.set_title(title, fontsize=11)
    ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=25)
    sns.despine(ax=ax)

axes[0].set_ylabel("Number of pairs")
fig.suptitle("Figure 11.1 \u00b7 Training set composition by tier", fontsize=13, y=1.02)
fig.tight_layout()
fig.savefig(REPO / "report" / "figures" / "fig11_1_training_composition.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

### v8: Disagreement Mining

The v8 pipeline ran the v7c ensemble on all 6,200 clean OpenCRE pairs and flagged 3,285 (53%) where the model's prediction disagreed with the OpenCRE-derived label. Of those, 2,000 were selected by highest uncertainty, and 673 were ultimately added to training—all labeled RELATED. The selection was conservative because GAT-based features (which require graph structure) cannot be computed for OpenCRE pairs: the external frameworks are not in our nine-framework graph. Instead, BGE-large cosine similarity served as the sole feature for these pairs, which limits stacking signal. The 673 additions shifted the class balance toward RELATED without adding any EQUIVALENT or PARTIAL pairs.

# Figure 11.2 — Disagreement mining funnel
funnel_labels = ["Clean pairs", "v7c disagrees", "Selected", "Added to\ntraining"]
funnel_values = [
    v8_diag["clean"],
    v8_diag["disagreements"],
    v8_diag["selected"],
    v8_diag["v8_rows_added"],
]
funnel_colors = ["#2a9d8f", "#e9c46a", "#f4a261", "#e76f51"]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.5))
bars = ax.bar(funnel_labels, funnel_values, color=funnel_colors, edgecolor="white", width=0.55)

# Count labels
for bar, val in zip(bars, funnel_values):
    ax.text(
        bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
        bar.get_height() + 80,
        f"{val:,}",
        ha="center", va="bottom", fontweight="bold", fontsize=11,
    )

# Percentage annotations between bars
for i in range(len(funnel_values) - 1):
    pct = funnel_values[i + 1] / funnel_values[i] * 100
    mid_x = (bars[i].get_x() + bars[i].get_width() / 2 + bars[i + 1].get_x() + bars[i + 1].get_width() / 2) / 2
    mid_y = (funnel_values[i] + funnel_values[i + 1]) / 2
    ax.annotate(
        f"{pct:.0f}%",
        xy=(mid_x, mid_y),
        fontsize=10, fontweight="bold", color="#264653",
        ha="center", va="center",
        bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.2", facecolor="white", edgecolor="#cccccc", alpha=0.8),
    )

ax.set_ylabel("Number of pairs")
ax.set_title("Figure 11.2 \u00b7 v8 disagreement mining funnel")
ax.set_ylim(0, max(funnel_values) * 1.2)
sns.despine()

fig.tight_layout()
fig.savefig(REPO / "report" / "figures" / "fig11_2_disagreement_mining.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

### v8b: Direct Augmentation and the Collapse Crisis

v8b took a broader approach: 2,046 OpenCRE pairs were added directly across three tiers (683 EQUIVALENT, 690 PARTIAL, 673 RELATED), capped per class to avoid further imbalance. Three transformer backbones were swept: RoBERTa-large, DeBERTa-large, and DeBERTa-base. DeBERTa-large collapsed—it produced 100% single-class predictions on the validation set, unable to learn any decision boundary from the noisy OpenCRE labels. The LightGBM stacker then memorized the small feature matrix: train_acc hit 100% while val_acc sat at 52.8%, a 47-point gap that confirms pure overfitting rather than any useful signal extraction.

# Figure 11.3 — Stacker overfitting diagnostic (from registry.jsonl)
registry = load_jsonl(REPO / "runs" / "registry.jsonl")

run_labels = ["v8b stacker", "v_final attempt 1", "v_final attempt 2"]
train_accs = [r["metrics"]["train_acc"] for r in registry]
val_accs   = [r["metrics"]["val_acc"] for r in registry]

x = np.arange(len(run_labels))
width = 0.32

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
bars_train = ax.bar(x - width / 2, train_accs, width, label="Train accuracy",
                     color="#2a9d8f", edgecolor="white")
bars_val   = ax.bar(x + width / 2, val_accs, width, label="Val accuracy",
                     color="#e76f51", edgecolor="white")

# Value labels
for bars in [bars_train, bars_val]:
    for bar in bars:
        ax.text(
            bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.015,
            f"{bar.get_height():.1%}",
            ha="center", va="bottom", fontweight="bold", fontsize=9,
        )

# Annotate the overfit run (index 0)
ax.annotate(
    "47-point gap\n100% train, 52.8% val",
    xy=(0, 1.0),
    xytext=(0.7, 0.88),
    fontsize=9, fontweight="bold", color="#e76f51",
    arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="->", lw=1.5, color="#e76f51"),
    ha="center", va="center",
    bbox=dict(boxstyle="round,pad=0.4", facecolor="#fff3f0", edgecolor="#e76f51", alpha=0.9),
)

ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(run_labels)
ax.set_ylabel("Accuracy")
ax.set_ylim(0, 1.15)
ax.set_title("Figure 11.3 \u00b7 Stacker overfitting diagnostic across registry runs")
ax.legend(loc="upper right")
sns.despine()

fig.tight_layout()
fig.savefig(REPO / "report" / "figures" / "fig11_3_stacker_overfitting.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()

### Takeaway

Two facts from v8/v8b shaped every decision in v_final: (1) DeBERTa-large collapses when trained on noisy OpenCRE labels—only DeBERTa-base and RoBERTa-large survive, and (2) a LightGBM stacker memorizes small feature matrices within 186 boosting rounds. Both failures demanded architectural changes, not more data.

> **Plain English:** We tried two ways to use the 6,200 clean OpenCRE pairs for training. v8 ran the existing model on those pairs, found 3,285 disagreements, and added 673 of the hardest cases—but only RELATED labels, creating a lopsided boost. v8b added 2,046 pairs across three tiers and swept three transformers. DeBERTa-large collapsed entirely, outputting a single class for every prediction. The stacker then overfit catastrophically: 100% training accuracy, 52.8% validation accuracy. These two failures proved that noisy external labels poison large models and that small feature matrices invite memorization. The result: v_final dropped OpenCRE from training and redesigned the stacking architecture from scratch.
